_session_intent_doc: skill-router v2 primer: maps a detected session signal (error/thrash/test_fail/git/explicit) to a curated phrase folded into the routing query, repeated `weight` times. Detection regex lives in session_ctx.py; only this phrase->weight mapping is data-tunable. Absent key -> built-in default. Weight hierarchy: error > thrash/test > git/explicit.

error.phrase: debug stacktrace traceback runtime crash logging breakpoint

thrash.phrase: code navigation find usages semantic search callers callees

test_fail.phrase: test failure debug coverage assertion regression

_demote_doc: Outcome-aware suppression, consumed by router_log.demotion(). blacklist = HARD cut: human-asserted noise that is never a real suggestion (a mode toggle active-by-default). data-driven demote (suggested >= min_suggested AND used == 0 in the settled .router-log.jsonl) applies the SOFT reversible `penalty` multiplier to the score — soft because the log's 'used' proxy undercounts Read-absorbed skills (ground-truth: ecc SKILL.md reads happen outside the router log), so any future logged use clears it. Tune min_suggested up to suppress less, penalty down to demote harder.
